title: Erysimum neglectum
intro: Erysimum neglectum, also known as Western wallflower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, as well as protect against cardiovascular diseases. The bioactive constituents of Erysimum neglectum include flavonoids, terpenes, and isothiocyanates, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Western wallflower is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Erysimum neglectum is classified within the Brassicaceae family, commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often found in temperate climates. Erysimum neglectum, being a member of this family, shares characteristics with other Brassicaceae members, such as having four-petaled flowers. The classification of Erysimum neglectum is further specified within the genus Erysimum, a group of flowering plants commonly known as wallflowers. The species Erysimum neglectum is native to North America, specifically in the western region.
